title: inula acinacifolia
intro: Inula acinacifolia, also known as the needle-leafed elecampane or needle-leaved inula, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which help to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The plant's medicinal properties make it beneficial for overall health, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Inula acinacifolia can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate colds and flu.
